summary: Postinstall pipes mutable-branch shell script to sh and sudo-installs unverified binary
details: On install, scripts/install.js executes `curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/neutron420/StackAudit/main/scripts/install.sh | sh` on Unix and `powershell iwr <main-branch URL> | iex` on Windows. The fetched install.sh queries the GitHub releases `latest` endpoint (not pinned to the npm package version 1.0.7), downloads a tarball, and runs `sudo mv stack /usr/local/bin/` with no hash or signature verification. Both the script source (mutable `main` branch) and the binary release (`latest` tag) are mutable references hosted outside the npm registry. Any future change to the repo's main branch or addition of a new GitHub release immediately propagates to every `npm install` of this package, with elevated privileges. Although the destination is the package publisher's own GitHub repo, the combination of mutable fetch + no integrity check + sudo write to /usr/local/bin matches the install-time-rce block shape: an attacker (or a compromised maintainer account) can ship arbitrary bytes to every installer's machine via a privileged path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
